payload.value: Anthropic will release Claude Opus 4 and Claude Sonnet 4, a new generation of Claude models with extended thinking, parallel tool execution, and agentic coding capabilities.

source.title: Introducing Claude 4 \ Anthropic
source.excerpt: Today we're releasing Claude Opus 4 and Claude Sonnet 4. Both models are available on our API, Amazon Bedrock, and Google Cloud's Vertex AI.

payload.value: Anthropic will make Claude Code generally available with VS Code and JetBrains extensions, a Claude Code SDK, and Claude Code on GitHub.

source.title: Introducing Claude 4 \ Anthropic
source.excerpt: Claude Code, now generally available, brings the power of Claude to more of your development workflow—in the terminal, your favorite IDEs, and running in the background with the Claude Code SDK.

payload.value: Anthropic will commit up to $100M in usage credits for Claude Mythos Preview to Project Glasswing partners for defensive cybersecurity work, plus $4M in donations to open-source security organizations.

source.title: Project Glasswing: Securing critical software for the AI era \ Anthropic
source.excerpt: Anthropic is committing up to $100M in usage credits for Mythos Preview across these efforts, as well as $4M in direct donations to open-source security organizations.

payload.value: Within 90 days of Project Glasswing launch (by ~July 12, 2026), Anthropic will report publicly on what was learned, vulnerabilities fixed, and improvements made that can be disclosed.

source.title: Project Glasswing: Securing critical software for the AI era \ Anthropic
source.excerpt: Within 90 days, Anthropic will report publicly on what we’ve learned, as well as the vulnerabilities fixed and improvements made that can be disclosed.

payload.value: Anthropic will release Claude Opus 4.7 with new cybersecurity safeguards (detecting and blocking prohibited cybersecurity uses), a Cyber Verification Program for legitimate security professionals, and new xhigh effort level.

source.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7 \ Anthropic
source.excerpt: Opus 4.7 is the first such model: its cyber capabilities are not as advanced as those of Mythos Preview... We are releasing Opus 4.7 with safeguards that automatically detect and block requests that indicate prohibited or high-risk cybersecurity uses.

payload.value: Anthropic's eventual goal is to enable users to safely deploy Mythos-class models at scale, contingent on developing cybersecurity safeguards; Mythos Preview will NOT be made generally available in the near term.

source.title: Project Glasswing: Securing critical software for the AI era \ Anthropic
source.excerpt: We do not plan to make Claude Mythos Preview generally available, but our eventual goal is to enable our users to safely deploy Mythos-class models at scale... We need to make progress in developing cybersecurity (and other) safeguards that detect and block the model’s most dangerous outputs.

source.title: Project Glasswing: Securing critical software for the AI era \ Anthropic

We need to make progress in developing cybersecurity (and other) safeguards that detect and block the model’s most dangerous outputs.

payload.value: OpenAI will open-source Symphony, a minimal orchestration spec and reference implementation for multi-agent coding workflows powered by Codex App Server.

source.title: An open-source spec for Codex orchestration: Symphony.
source.excerpt: We are open sourcing Symphony to demonstrate the power of Codex App Server when paired with different workflow tools, like Linear... Symphony garnered over 15K GitHub stars as of April 23.

payload.value: OpenAI will open-source datasets and reference code from its chain-of-thought monitorability evaluation suite, and will report monitorability results in all future frontier reasoning model system cards.

source.title: Open Sourcing Monitorability Evaluations
source.excerpt: We are open-sourcing datasets and reference code from our work on evaluating chain-of-thought monitorability... As long as CoT monitorability remains an important layer in our AGI safety strategy, we will report results on monitorability evaluations in future frontier reasoning model system cards.

payload.value: xAI will release grok-voice-think-fast-1.0, a flagship voice agent model available via API, supporting 25+ languages and real-time reasoning with no added latency.

source.title: Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 | xAI
source.excerpt: Today, we’re excited to announce a step change in xAI’s Voice Agent capabilities: Introducing grok-voice-think-fast-1.0 — our new flagship voice model... Our most capable voice agent is now available via API.

payload.value: Mistral AI will release Mistral Medium 3.5 open weights under a modified MIT license on Hugging Face, a dense 128B model supporting 256k context with configurable reasoning effort.

payload.value: Mistral AI will release Leanstral weights under an Apache 2.0 license as the first open-source code agent for Lean 4, along with a free API endpoint and a tech report and FLTEval evaluation suite.

source.title: Leanstral: Open-Source foundation for trustworthy vibe-coding | Mistral AI
source.excerpt: We release Leanstral, the first open-source code agent designed for Lean 4... We release Leanstral weights under an Apache 2.0 license, in an agent mode within Mistral vibe, and through a free API endpoint.

payload.value: Mistral AI and NVIDIA, as founding members of the NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition, plan to co-develop frontier open-source AI models; the coalition's first initiative is a base model trained on NVIDIA DGX Cloud that will underpin the upcoming NVIDIA Nemotron 4 family, to be open-sourced.

source.title: Mistral AI partners with NVIDIA to accelerate open frontier models | Mistral AI
source.excerpt: Mistral AI and NVIDIA plan to co-develop frontier open-source AI models... The coalition’s first initiative is a base model, trained on NVIDIA DGX Cloud, which will underpin the upcoming NVIDIA Nemotron 4 family. These models will be open-sourced.

payload.value: OpenAI will make GPT-5.5 and other frontier models available on Amazon Bedrock via an expanded AWS partnership, alongside Codex on AWS and Amazon Bedrock Managed Agents powered by OpenAI, launching in limited preview.

source.title: OpenAI models, Codex, and Managed Agents come to AWS | OpenAI
source.excerpt: For many companies, using AI at scale requires bringing the best models to the systems their teams already use. That's why we're launching OpenAI models, including our best frontier model GPT-5.5, on Amazon Bedrock.

source.title: OpenAI models, Codex, and Managed Agents come to AWS | OpenAI

payload.value: Anthropic will release a set of creative-tool connectors for Claude, integrating with Blender, Autodesk Fusion, Adobe Creative Cloud, Ableton, Splice, Affinity by Canva, Resolume, and SketchUp, enabling Claude to work directly within creative workflows.

source.title: Claude for Creative Work \ Anthropic
source.excerpt: Today, with a coalition of partners including Blender, Autodesk, Adobe, Ableton, and Splice, we're releasing a set of connectors—tools that let Claude work alongside the software creative professionals rely on.

payload.value: Mistral AI will release Workflows in public preview — a durable, observable enterprise AI orchestration layer with human-in-the-loop approvals, fault tolerance, and Python-based workflow authoring integrated into Mistral Studio and Le Chat.

source.title: Workflows for work that runs the business | Mistral AI
source.excerpt: Workflows is now in public preview. Today, we're releasing Workflows in public preview. Workflows is the orchestration layer for enterprise AI.

payload.value: OpenAI (via The Stargate Project) committed to securing 10GW of AI infrastructure in the United States by 2029, beginning with an immediate $100B deployment.

source.title: Announcing The Stargate Project | OpenAI
source.excerpt: The Stargate Project is a new company which intends to invest $500 billion over the next four years building new AI infrastructure for OpenAI in the United States. We will begin deploying $100 billion immediately.

source.title: Announcing The Stargate Project | OpenAI
source.excerpt: The Stargate Project is a new company which intends to invest $500 billion over the next four years building new AI infrastructure for OpenAI in the United States. We will begin deploying $100 billion immediately.

source.title: Building the compute infrastructure for the Intelligence Age | OpenAI
source.excerpt: When we announced Stargate in January 2025, we committed to securing 10GW of AI infrastructure in the United States by 2029. Just over a year later, we have already surpassed that milestone, with more than 3GW added in the last 90 days alone.
